steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Rao claims that eliminating left turns at busy intersections improves overall traffic flow and reduces total travel times — even when drivers must take slightly longer routes. Supporting evidence must show that, after eliminating left turns, people can actually get more done or travel more efficiently overall, not just that individual turns are faster or slower.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "After a city eliminated left turns at busy intersections, a package delivery company reports that its drivers have been able to reach more addresses in the city daily, on average, and therefore deliver more packages there annually.": a delivery company reports higher daily delivery counts after the city eliminated left turns. More addresses reached per day means drivers are moving through the city faster overall — exactly what Rao's claim predicts. ✓
  • In a town that installed left-turn signals at all busy intersections, seven out of ten survey respondents agreed with the statement “the streets in my community are easier to navigate by motor vehicle than before.”: this tests adding left-turn signals, not eliminating left turns. It addresses the opposite intervention and says nothing about whether elimination improves efficiency. ✗
  • "A traffic study of intersections in a large city shows that on average drivers wait longer to make a left turn at intersections without left-turn signals than at intersections with such signals.": this only measures wait time at individual left-turn intersections, not overall travel efficiency. It also doesn't involve eliminating left turns at all. ✗
  • "Statistics reveal that school buses in a city that eliminated left turns at most intersections took on average two minutes longer to complete their routes after the restriction took effect than they did before.": longer route times directly contradict Rao's claim that travel times improve. This would weaken, not support, the claim. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
